domain: Pit viper loreal organ (infrared snake heat sensor): a membrane in hollow chamber detects thermal radiation as DEVIATION from local thermal background; dual-air-contact provides cool-side reference; the sensor reports DIFFERENCE = target - ambient at the receptor level.
specific_mechanism: Sensor reports DIFFERENCE = warm IR target - local ambient thermal baseline (computed at membrane level via dual-air-contact); baseline is continuously updated PHYSICAL reference, not a memory state.
llm_application: PIT-VIPER differential signal evaluation diagnostic: for benchmark B, evaluate candidate L_c AND a fixed TRIVIAL baseline model L_b (constant-output, random-class, fixed-prompt) on the SAME queries. Report per-query D_q = score(L_c, q) - score(L_b, q), then aggregate D as the primary diagnostic. Per-query differential identifies candidates that score marginally above trivial baseline (small D_q) → flag as 'not genuinely informative' on that subset of B. Trivial baseline updates per-task to reflect benchmark-specific 'thermal background' (e.g., random for 4-choice MCQ has 0.25; majority-class on imbalanced dataset has empirical priori; fixed-prompt for generation has its own bigram score). Differs from (a) accuracy-against-global-baseline (dataset-level, not per-query), (b) Brier decomposition (probabilistic, not paired-subtraction), (c) Elo (head-to-head models not vs trivial), (d) calibration error (probability accuracy, not differential). Recombines (i) PER-QUERY paired subtraction + (ii) FIXED-TRIVIAL baseline model + (iii) DIFFERENTIAL diagnostic aggregate.
novelty_claim: Item Response Theory uses calibration. Trivial-baseline ablation reports dataset-level. Elo uses head-to-head. PIT-VIPER recombines (a) PER-QUERY paired differential + (b) FIXED-TRIVIAL baseline model + (c) per-task recalibrated trivial. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
